info.title: Astrodynamics command line interface
info.description: This CLI allows end user to exploit IO.Astrodynamics framework

commands.name: angular-separation
commands.description: Compute angular separation between given objects

options.name: --to-state-vector
options.description: Display result as state vector (Default: True)

commands.name: find-windows-from-coordinate-constraint
commands.description: Find time windows when constraint occurs

commands.name: find-windows-from-distance-constraint
commands.description: Find time windows when constraint occurs

commands.name: find-windows-from-FOV-constraint
commands.description: Find time windows when a target is in field of view of given instrument

commands.name: find-windows-from-illumination-constraint
commands.description: Find time windows when constraint occurs

commands.name: find-windows-from-occultation-constraint
commands.description: Find time windows when constraint occurs

commands.name: frame-converter
commands.description: Convert a frame to another at given epoch

commands.name: orbital-parameters-converter
commands.description: Convert an orbital parameters to another type at given epoch in given frame

options.name: --from-state-vector
options.description: Orbital parameters values represents a state vector

options.name: --from-keplerian
options.description: Orbital parameters values represents keplerian elements

options.name: --from-equinoctial
options.description: Orbital parameters values represents equinoctial elements

options.name: --from-tle
options.description: Orbital parameters values represents two lines elements

options.name: --from-state-vector
options.description: Orbital parameters values represents a state vector

options.name: --from-keplerian
options.description: Orbital parameters values represents keplerian elements

options.name: --from-equinoctial
options.description: Orbital parameters values represents equinoctial elements

options.name: --from-tle
options.description: Orbital parameters values represents two lines elements

options.name: --use-atmospheric-drag
options.description: Include atmospheric drag perturbation (Earth and Mars only)

options.name: --earth-geopotential-degree
options.description: Number of degrees used by earth geopotential model (Max. 100 (Default: 10)

commands.name: sub-point
commands.description: Compute sub observer point of given object in planetodetic, planetocentric or planetocentric cartesian coordinates

options.name: --cartesian
options.description: Display result as planetocentric cartesian coordinates

commands.name: time-converter
commands.description: Convert a time system to another

options.name: --to-seconds-from-j2000
options.description: Convert to elapsed seconds from J2000 epoch
